summary: Verified the login screen layout fix for Alô Clínica (Expo/React Native web preview). The blue/orange hero no longer covers the form card. All required elements are visible for both patient and doctor roles. Login navigation to /home works. All specified testIDs (btn-back, input-email, input-password, btn-login, btn-google, btn-apple, link-register) are present and visible. Fix is confirmed working.

react_native_issues.issue: Console warning: '"shadow*" style props are deprecated. Use "boxShadow"'. Non-blocking but should be migrated in RN 0.75+. Reference: SHADOW.card in /app/frontend/src/theme (indirect via login.tsx:225,271)
Console warning: 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents'. Likely from Pressable/Animated wrappers. Non-blocking.

action_items: Optional: Replace SHADOW.card style props with boxShadow syntax to remove deprecation warnings.
Optional: Migrate props.pointerEvents to style.pointerEvents on affected wrappers.
critical_code_review_comments: login.tsx (304 lines) is within acceptable size; StyleSheet.create used correctly.
All interactive elements have testID (btn-back, input-email, input-password, btn-login, btn-google, btn-apple, link-register).

Hero padding (SPACING.xl) + scroll paddingTop (SPACING.lg) + card marginTop:0 result in a clean, non-overlapping layout at 375x667. 'Entrar na conta' title bounding box (y=334) sits well below hero bottom.
Login handler is MOCKED (setTimeout → router.replace), which is expected per problem statement.
Pingo mascot renders with animation; no clipping observed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Login screen layout is verified for both patient (blue hero, camila@email.com) and doctor (orange hero, ana@aloclinica.com) roles at mobile viewport 375x667. Both flows navigate to /home on Entrar click. Pre-filled password: 123456. Deep-linking via /login?role=doctor works. Screenshots stored in /app/test_reports/screenshots/.
rca_of_issue: Original: ScrollView + card marginTop:-70 caused negative-margin content to be clipped above the ScrollView's origin, hiding the top of the form card behind the hero. Fix (hero paddingBottom:SPACING.xl, card marginTop:0, scroll paddingTop:SPACING.lg, smaller Pingo & title) is confirmed effective — hero ends cleanly above the card and all form elements are visible.
